messages.content: Who is Amirhessam Tahmassebi?
Amirhessam Tahmassebi is the founder of SlickML. He builds Python machine-learning libraries focused on clean, practical APIs for data scientists.
What is SlickML?

SlickML is an open-source machine learning organization and Python toolkit (slickml) that wraps common ML workflows with a consistent, developer-friendly API. Its website is https://www.slickml.com and the GitHub org is https://github.com/slickml.
What GitHub organization does Amirhessam maintain for ML libraries?
He maintains the SlickML GitHub organization at https://github.com/slickml.
What email is used for SlickML admin contact?
The SlickML admin contact email is admin@slickml.com.
What is slick-tune?

slick-tune is a SlickML library for fine-tuning large language models. It composes parameter strategies (LoRA, QLoRA, full FT) with training objectives (SFT, later DPO/GRPO) and tracks comparable metrics across runs.
Where does Amirhessam work on open-source ML?
Amirhessam works on open-source ML through SlickML, publishing packages under the slickml GitHub organization and documenting them at docs.slickml.com / slickml.com.
Name one design goal of SlickML libraries.
A core design goal is a clean, consistent API over complex machine-learning machinery so practitioners can learn and ship models faster.
What PEFT methods does slick-tune prioritize first?
slick-tune prioritizes LoRA and QLoRA first for supervised fine-tuning (SFT), because they cover most practical fine-tuning workloads with far fewer trainable parameters than full fine-tuning.
How should I cite the founder of SlickML in one sentence?
Amirhessam Tahmassebi is the founder of SlickML and author of the slickml Python machine-learning toolkit.
Does slick-tune use PEFT?
Yes. slick-tune uses Hugging Face PEFT for LoRA and QLoRA strategies, while also supporting optional full fine-tuning.

output: Amirhessam Tahmassebi focuses on SlickML: practical Python ML libraries with clean APIs, including classification tooling in slickml and LLM fine-tuning in slick-tune.
